figures.caption: Table 1: This table reports suite-averaged offline SAE diagnostics and closed-loop fidelity for each policy stream and layer, with means ± standard deviations taken across the four LIBERO suites. Raw SR is the no-hook policy success rate; Hooked SR is the success rate when the SAE reconstruction replaces the hidden state at the corresponding layer. PG layer 17 is omitted because the final PG prefix output is discarded after KV-cache construction.

Table 7: Per-feature ∆SR = SRα=0 − SRbaseline (percentage points) for π0.5 zero-out interventions, broken down across the top-3 features per ranking and averaged over the four LIBERO suites. (a) PG drops stay within roughly 5 pp across all rows. (b) AE event-aligned, window-mean, and task-mean entries cluster near the policy-collapse floor (≈ −96), leaving the within-ranking ordering largely indistinguishable.

Figure 12: This figure shows per-feature ∆SR (percentage points) under zero-out for the three SAE streams. Left: OpenVLA layer 31 (top-1 to top-5). Middle: π0.5 PG, layer-averaged across {0, 5, 11, 16} (top-1 to top-3). Right: π0.5 AE, layer-averaged across {0, 5, 11, 17} (top-1 to top-3). Each panel uses an independent y-scale to preserve within-stream structure.

Table 3: Closed-loop SR after zeroing selected SAE features. Mean SR (%) is followed in parentheses by ∆SR = SRα=0 − SRbaseline in percentage points (negative indicates lower success). Each entry averages over the top K features per ranking.

Table 2: Keyframe and event-cluster statistics. Keyframes/rollout: AWE-proposed kinematic keyframes per rollout. Clusters: total task-local event clusters per suite. Recurring: subset passing the 50% episode-coverage filter, feeding suite-level rankings (§3.4).

Figure 13: Qualitative comparison on LIBERO-Object pick up the cream cheese and place it in the basket (f24287, layer 31, α = 150). Top: baseline succeeds. Middle: decoder-vector steering drives the arm far off-task, then locks into that pose. Bottom: matched random-vector control produces erratic off-task motion.

Figure 1: This figure shows the 4 stages of the event-grounded SAE pipeline: (1) SAE training (Sec. 3.1); (2) kinematic keyframe extraction (Sec. 3.2); (3) task-local event clustering with VLM annotation (Sec. 3.3); (4) closed-loop validation through residual-preserving latent edits (Sec. 3.5).

Figure 9: Four representative OpenVLA task-local event clusters; each row shows one keyframe bundle as a five-frame strip at offsets {−4,−2, 0, 2, 4} around the keyframe timestep, with the cluster’s VLM-assigned phrase, phase label, and episode-coverage fraction.

Figure 14: Per-rank single-feature zero-out closed-loop success rate for resid_post versus mlp_out at OpenVLA LIBERO-Spatial layer 31; the dashed line marks the no-intervention baseline. The matched protocol and the comparison are described in the text.

Table 5: Reproducibility hyperparameters. (a) SAE training (size denotes activation dimension → dictionary size). (b) Keyframe extraction and event clustering; the three weights apply to the descriptor concatenation in Section 3.3, where each component is normalized before weighting and the concatenated descriptor is ℓ2-normalized.
Figure 4: Simulator dose-response on LIBERO-10: π0.5 action-expert per-layer ∆SR (percentage points) as αf sweeps from 0 (zero-out) to 1 (identity), one line per ranking.

Figure 11: Additional paired rollout snapshots for suite-level zero-out; each row ablates one eventcluster-selected layer-31 SAE feature. Failures range from localized degradation (poor target localization, mistimed gripper) to larger control changes (e.g., LIBERO-Goal turn on the stove with feature f16646 induces markedly larger arm excursions).

Figure 8: Event-aligned feature score heatmaps (OpenVLA, four LIBERO suites): rows are event clusters, columns are top layer-31 SAE features, cell color is the event-aligned score; side strips show task identity and phase labels.

Figure 5: Real hardware dose-response: end-effector distance to the prompted chip cluster vs SAEsuppression strength at three AE layers. Lower distance means the rollout follows the prompt more faithfully; the shaded band is the no-perturbation range.

Table 6: This table reports closed-loop success after randomly dropping 16 active PaliGemma SAE features. Values are percentages. Dropout SR reports the mean success rate ± standard deviation across three random seeds. ∆SR is computed as SRdropout − SRbaseline in percentage points. Negative values indicate lower success after intervention.
Figure 6: Chip-approach setup with counterbalanced red/yellow piles; orange dashed arrow shows a perturbed trajectory.
